name: Pierce_Mech

infobox.Systems: AP Cannon(Brute)EffectFire a pushing projectile that pierces the first target and damages the second.(2 damage)Upgrade I+1 Damage ()Increases damage to the second hit tile by 1.Upgrade II+1 Damage ()Increases damage to the second hit tile by 1.

sections.Team: The mech is available as part of theBombermechssquad.
sections.Strategy:
The Pierce Mech starts with excellent stats, having a projectile weapon that deals 2 damage. The pushing capabilities and damage output are similar to those of theCombat Mech, but with greater range. However, this comes at the expense of needing to shoot through another target or structure first.
In addition to units, the AP Cannon can also pierce grid buildings and mountains. Deployable units such as Bomblings, Arachnoids and Tanks can also be pierced, either before or after they move. The requirement to pierce is not always a drawback; since the pierced target is also pushed, look for opportunities to displace two enemies at once, or to push an ally in a better position with the first push.
If three or more enemy units are lined up and adjacent, this weapon can also deal substantial knock damage to multiple targets. To put this in perspective, with no upgrades to AP Cannon; the first enemy will be pushed into the second one for 1 bump damage to each, the second unit will take 2 damage and be pushed onto the third enemy, which will then take additional bump damage of 1 to each. That's a total of 4 damage alone on the second target, and 1 damage to the first and third unit. This is most useful againstAlpha Diggerenemies; the Pierce Mech can use its protective rocks for a pierce target and bump damage to take it out in one hit, even with no damage upgrades.
The potential damage of AP Cannon is overall high, reaching a maximum of 4 with upgrades before bump damage is considered. This can become expensive at 4 total Reactor Cores (2 per+1 Damageupgrade), so the full upgrades are best considered for 3 or 4 island playthroughs.
4 base move is reasonably mobile to start; but due to the heavy positional requirements, the Pierce Mech benefits from a pilot that offers additional mobility, such asHenry Kwan,Prospero, or one with additional movement as an ability.
